completed_tasks.name: Implement SIGSTOP/SIGCONT live pause and resume with process-level tests
Verify Docker same-PID and byte-continuity behavior
Verify OpenWrt 23.05 and 24.10 and update documentation

decisions.decision: Use SIGSTOP and SIGCONT on the worker-owned Popen object for live pause and continue.
decisions.rationale: This preserves the same process, open file descriptor, and byte offset while avoiding stale or reused database PID signaling.

decisions.decision: Keep tdl --continue only for completed-item recovery after failed or canceled runs.
decisions.rationale: tdl 0.20.3 does not resume byte ranges inside a partially downloaded single file.

decisions.decision: Continue a stopped child before SIGINT during cancel or service shutdown.
decisions.rationale: A stopped process cannot handle SIGINT until it receives SIGCONT.

next_action: Review commit 4b4cb74, then push or package it for release if desired.
context_notes: Implementation is complete. Local unittest discovery passed 112 tests with two Windows-only POSIX permission skips. Docker E2E proved PID 21 remained unchanged, bytes stayed fixed while stopped, and increased after continue. OpenWrt 23.05.6 and 24.10.2 process-level E2E tests passed. The two Docker test containers are healthy on ports 19910 and 19911 and all six named volumes remain intact.
